question:
What happens?

```sql
SELECT * FROM employees
WHERE salary = (SELECT salary FROM employees WHERE department_id = 50);
```
options: Runs fine always
Error if the subquery returns more than one row
Returns all employees
Returns no rows

explanation.uz: `=` bilan bir qatorli (single-row) ichki so'rov kutiladi. Agar ichki so'rov bir nechta satr qaytarsa, ORA-01427 "single-row subquery returns more than one row" xatosi chiqadi. Bunda `IN` ishlatish kerak.
explanation.en: `=` expects a single-row subquery. If the subquery returns more than one row, you get ORA-01427 "single-row subquery returns more than one row". Use `IN` in that case.

question:
What does > ALL mean?

```sql
WHERE salary > ALL (SELECT salary FROM employees WHERE department_id = 30)
```
options: Greater than the MINIMUM of the subquery values
Greater than the MAXIMUM of the subquery values
Greater than the average
Equal to all values

explanation.uz: `> ALL` — barcha qiymatlardan katta, ya'ni ular ichidagi ENG KATTASIDAN ham katta bo'lishi kerak.
explanation.en: `> ALL` means greater than every value, i.e. greater than the MAXIMUM of them.

question:
What does > ANY mean?

```sql
WHERE salary > ANY (SELECT salary FROM employees WHERE department_id = 30)
```
options: Greater than the MINIMUM of the subquery values
Greater than the MAXIMUM of the subquery values
Greater than all values
Equal to any value

explanation.uz: `> ANY` — qiymatlardan kamida bittasidan katta, ya'ni ular ichidagi ENG KICHIGIDAN katta bo'lsa yetarli.
explanation.en: `> ANY` means greater than at least one of the values, i.e. greater than the MINIMUM of them.

question: What is the difference between UNION and UNION ALL?
options: No difference
UNION removes duplicates (and sorts); UNION ALL keeps all rows
UNION keeps duplicates; UNION ALL removes them
UNION ALL only works with numbers

explanation.uz: `UNION` ikki natijani birlashtirib, takroriy satrlarni olib tashlaydi (va saralaydi). `UNION ALL` esa hamma satrni (takrorlar bilan) qaytaradi — tezroq, chunki dublikatlarni tekshirmaydi.
explanation.en: `UNION` combines two results and removes duplicates (and sorts). `UNION ALL` keeps all rows (including duplicates) — faster because it doesn't check for duplicates.

question:
What does the MINUS operator return?

```sql
SELECT id FROM a
MINUS
SELECT id FROM b;
```
options: Rows in both a and b
Rows in a that are NOT in b
Rows in b that are not in a
All rows from a and b

explanation.uz: `MINUS` birinchi so'rovда bo'lgan, lekin ikkinchisida bo'lmagan satrlarni qaytaradi (a dan b ni ayirish). Takroriy satrlar ham olib tashlanadi.
explanation.en: `MINUS` returns rows that are in the first query but NOT in the second (a minus b). Duplicates are also removed.

question: What does EXISTS check in a correlated subquery?
options: Whether the subquery returns at least one row
The exact count of rows
Whether a column is NULL
The maximum value

explanation.uz: `EXISTS` ichki so'rov kamida BITTA satr qaytaradimi yo'qmi shuni tekshiradi (TRUE/FALSE). Aniq qiymat yoki son muhim emas — faqat satr bor-yo'qligi.
explanation.en: `EXISTS` checks whether the subquery returns AT LEAST one row (TRUE/FALSE). The actual values or counts don't matter — only whether any row exists.
